skill.name: multi-source-news-writer
skill.description: Integrates multiple news sources into a single, cohesive press release using an inverted pyramid structure and AP style. Use when you have raw news content and a topic, and need a professional press release.
skill.summary: Synthesizes multiple news sources into one professional press release using inverted pyramid structure and AP style guidelines.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 39 static analysis findings are false positives after manual review. The 23 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are Markdown code fences in .md files showing example bash commands, not actual shell execution. The 6 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are plain text patterns such as 'max 20 characters' character limits and argparse description strings, not cryptographic operations. The 7 'System reconnaissance' findings are skill description text reused in audit result labels and YAML frontmatter. The 2 'Hardcoded URL' findings are a legitimate GitHub source repository citation. This is a legitimate press release writing skill with no executable threats, no network exfiltration, no command injection vectors, and no obfuscated intent.

content.user_title: Write Press Releases from Multiple News Sources
content.value_statement: This skill helps you combine information from multiple news sources into a single professional press release. It applies inverted pyramid structure and AP style for clear, objective writing.
actual_capabilities: Integrates information from multiple news sources into one press release
Applies inverted pyramid structure with headline, lead, and body sections
Extracts key facts (5W1H: who, what, when, where, why, how) from source materials
Resolves contradictory information across sources with verification notes
Follows AP style guidelines with objective tone and active voice
Produces structured Markdown output ready for publication
limitations: Requires all source text to be provided as input; cannot fetch news from the web
Does not verify facts or claims against external sources
Character limits per section are guidelines, not hard enforcement
May not capture nuanced context from highly technical or domain-specific content

use_cases.title: Corporate Press Release Drafting
use_cases.description: A communications professional combines multiple internal memos and reports into a single press release about a company product launch.

use_cases.title: News Roundup Consolidation
use_cases.description: A journalist reviews coverage from several news outlets on the same event and creates a consolidated, balanced summary for publication.

use_cases.title: Research Finding Announcement
use_cases.description: A researcher synthesizes findings from multiple study summaries and press briefings into an announcement for a scientific discovery.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Press Release from Single Source
prompt_templates.prompt:
Use the multi-source-news-writer skill to create a press release from this news content:

[Paste news article or press release text here]

Topic: [Enter the main topic]
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting point for users new to the skill with a single news source

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Source Consolidation
prompt_templates.prompt:
Use the multi-source-news-writer skill to synthesize these sources into one press release:

Source 1:
[Paste source text]

Source 2:
[Paste source text]

Source 3:
[Paste source text]

Topic: [Enter the main topic]
prompt_templates.scenario: Combining multiple sources covering the same event or topic

prompt_templates.title: Press Release with Specific Angle
prompt_templates.prompt:
Use the multi-source-news-writer skill. Write a press release focused on [specific angle or audience]. Sources:

[Paste source texts]

Topic: [Enter topic]

Emphasize: [Specific aspect to highlight]
prompt_templates.scenario: Tailoring the press release toward a specific audience or angle

prompt_templates.title: Conflict Resolution and Fact-Check Notes
prompt_templates.prompt:
Use the multi-source-news-writer skill. These sources contain conflicting information:

Source A:
[Paste source text]

Source B:
[Paste source text]

Topic: [Enter topic]

Please mark all contradictions and note which points need verification.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use case where sources contradict each other on key facts

output_examples.input: Multiple news articles about a new renewable energy project launch
output_examples.output:
## SolarGrid Launches Largest Offshore Wind Farm in Southeast Asia

### Singapore-based SolarGrid Energy has opened the 500MW Pacific Breeze wind farm off the coast of Vietnam, marking the largest renewable energy project in the region.

The project created 3,000 construction jobs and will power an estimated 200,000 homes. Energy analysts project the farm will reduce regional carbon emissions by 400,000 tons annually. The Vietnamese government has committed to additional renewable energy incentives following the launch.

output_examples.input: Company merger announcement from two conflicting press releases
output_examples.output:
## Apex Dynamics and CoreTech Industries Announce Merger Agreement

### Apex Dynamics and CoreTech Industries have signed a definitive merger agreement, creating a combined entity valued at approximately $12 billion.

Apex reports the merger will complete by Q3 2026, while CoreTech states final regulatory approval is still pending. Both companies expect the combined entity to achieve $200 million in annual cost synergies within two years. Employee impact remains unconfirmed; Apex has not disclosed workforce plans.

best_practices: Provide complete source text as input for the most accurate synthesis and fact extraction
Specify the target audience or publication outlet to match tone and level of detail
Review the output for fact discrepancies and add verification notes for conflicting information
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for creative writing or opinion pieces that require subjective voice
Do not rely on this skill to verify factual accuracy of source material against external references
Do not paste truncated or partial source text, as this leads to incomplete synthesis

faq.question: What is the inverted pyramid structure?
faq.answer: The inverted pyramid places the most important information first, followed by supporting details, and then background. It starts with a headline, then a lead paragraph, and finally body paragraphs with descending importance.

faq.question: Does this skill fetch news from the internet?
faq.answer: No. You must provide all source text directly as input. The skill only processes the text you supply in your prompt.

faq.question: What is AP style?
faq.answer: AP style is a set of writing guidelines used by news organizations. It emphasizes short sentences, active voice, objective tone, and consistent formatting.

faq.question: Can I use this for scientific or technical announcements?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill works with any topic. Provide the source material and specify your topic to generate a press release.

faq.question: How does the skill handle conflicting information from different sources?
faq.answer: The skill marks contradictory information and notes which points need verification. It does not fabricate resolutions for conflicts in source material.

faq.question: What output format does the skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill produces a Markdown document with headline, lead paragraph, and body sections organized by the inverted pyramid structure.
